# Vision

## 1. Problem

What is broken in the world today that this product fixes? Who feels the pain?

## 2. Primary user

Who is the single most important user? Describe them concretely — their role, their context, the moment they reach for this product.

## 3. Job to be done

When `<situation>`, the user wants to `<motivation>` so they can `<expected outcome>`.

## 4. Alternatives today

What does the user use today instead? Why is it insufficient?

## 5. Why now

What changed in technology, market, or regulation that makes this possible/necessary today and not 3 years ago?

## 6. Success criteria

Three to five measurable outcomes that prove this product worked. Not features — outcomes.

## 7. Non-goals

What this product explicitly will NOT do, even though it's tempting. Drawing this line up-front prevents drift.

## 8. Scope boundaries

What's in v1, what's in v2, what's "someday/maybe", and what's never.

## 9. Key risks

Top 3-5 things that could kill this. Technical, market, regulatory, organizational.

## 10. Stakeholders

Name them. For each: their interest, their influence, their veto power if any.

## 11. Open questions

Things you don't know yet. Owned by named people with target resolution dates.

---

**Traceability:** FRs link upward to one or more numbered sections above. NFRs link upward to sections 1, 2, 6, and 9.
